zh.lifeSummary: 當William Bond於1493年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍時，他的父親William Bond II of Somerset18歲，母親Jane Gibbs12歲。他於1523年5月與Dionese Bowerman在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1549年12月14日之前在英國薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍。

nameMeanings.name: Bond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

variants.name: Bundock
meanings: English (Essex): from a Middle English personal name Bondoc, a pet form of Bonde (late Old English Bonda; see Bond 1) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -oc. Compare Bundick .

variants.name: Husband
meanings: English and Scottish (Fife): status name from Middle English housbond(e) ‘male spouse’, ‘householder, male head of a household’, ‘villein or customary tenant who holds land additional to the croft attached to his homestead’, ‘husbandman or farmer’. The term (late Old English hūsbonda, Old Norse húsbóndi) is a compound of hús ‘house’ + bóndi (see Bond ).

variants.name: Bonde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik.
English (Devon): variant of   Bond  .
Danish and Swedish: status name for a farmer, from Old Norse bóndi ‘farmer’ (see also Bond ).

variants.name: Bunde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Gerhard, Hans, Kurt.
North German: status name for a farmer from Middle Low German bunde, bunne, related to Danish, Swedish bonde ‘free peasant’ (see Bond ).

German (Bünde): topographic name from Old High German biunda ‘enclosed fertile pasture’ (modern German Bünde, Beunte), a derivative of biwenden ‘to bend or weave’, with reference to the practice of making fences from woven branches. There are many different local forms and communal implications concerning the use of the land.

variants.name: Bondarenko
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Oleg, Vladimir, Yuriy, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Anatoly, Gennady, Grigoriy, Igor, Konstantin, Lyubov, Lyudmila.
Ukrainian: patronymic from bondar ‘cooper’, formed with the diminutive suffix -enko. Compare Bond .

variants.name: Bonds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bounds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bound
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bundy
meanings: variant of Bond , either from Middle English bonde, bounde ‘customary tenant’ where the final -e has developed in local speech to -y, or occasionally from the Old Norse Bondi, where final -i has survived.
alternatively, a variant of   Bandy  .
English:

variants.name: Band
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When William Bond was born in 1493, in West Buckland, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Bond II of Somerset, was 18 and his mother, Jane Gibbs, was 12. He married Dionese Bowerman in May 1523, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 7 daughters. He died before 14 December 1549, in West Buckland, Somerset, England, and was buried in West Buckland, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Bond est né en 1493, à West Buckland, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Bond II of Somerset, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Jane Gibbs, avait 12 ans. Il a épousé Dionese Bowerman en mai 1523, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé avant 14 décembre 1549, à West Buckland, Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterré à West Buckland, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lui sposato Dionese Bowerman nel maggio 1523, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 7 figlie. Morì prima del 14 dicembre 1549, nel West Buckland, Somerset, England, e fu sepolto a West Buckland, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Bondが1493年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトウエスト ブックランドで生まれた時，父William Bond II of Somersetは18歳，母Jane Gibbsは12歳であった。1523年5月にイギリスデボンでDionese Bowermanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1549年12月14日以前にイギリスソメルセトウエスト ブックランドで死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトウエスト ブックランドにて埋葬された。

ko.Buckland (St Mary), Somersetshire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 벅랜드 스트 메리

ko.lifeSummary: William Bond(이)가 1493년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Bond II of Somerset(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Gibbs(은)는 12세였다. 그는 1523년 5월 영국 데폰에서 Dionese Bowerman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1549년 12월 14일 이전 영국 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Bond nasceu em 1493, em West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Bond II of Somerset, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Jane Gibbs, tinha 12 anos. Ele casou-se com Dionese Bowerman em maio de 1523, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 14 de dezembro de 1549, em West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Buckland (St Mary), Somersetshire. Engla: буккланд ст мэри, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Bond родился в 1493 г. в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Bond II of Somerset было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Gibbs, 12. Он женился на женщине по имени Dionese Bowerman май 1523 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер перед 14 декабрь 1549 г. в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия и был похоронен в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Bond nació en 1493, en West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Bond II of Somerset, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Jane Gibbs, tenía 12 años. Se casó con Dionese Bowerman en mayo de 1523, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió antes de 14 de diciembre de 1549, en West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
